item.title: Could you be the FT’s new stock picking champion?
item.description: Register your portfolio now — the 8-week competition starts on 9 June

item.title: Bitcoin tumbles after Strategy sale unnerves crypto traders
item.description: Cryptocurrency is heading for biggest weekly loss since November 2022

item.title: Chips sell-off threatens Wall Street’s winning streak
item.description: Tech stocks suffer two-day decline following disappointing results from semiconductor giant Broadcom

item.title: Mike Ashley’s Frasers seeks Big Four auditor after push to improve governance
item.description: Retail group has tried to professionalise after previously struggling to find a firm to sign off its accounts

item.title: Pugilistic Florida billionaire plots law firm stake sale to private equity
item.description: Move by largest US personal injury firm could cement buyout groups’ arrival in a sector that has long been off-limits

item.title: Stray Ukrainian sea drone explodes in Romanian port
item.description: Kyiv has blamed Russian jamming for its air and sea drones veering off into Nato countries

item.title: UK retail investor demand for SpaceX surges to tens of thousands
item.description: More than 30,000 small investors register interest in hotly awaited IPO

item.title: BBC director-general Matt Brittin: ‘It’s worth fighting for’
item.description: The former Google executive on the challenges facing the public service broadcaster, his plans to cut 2,000 jobs — and what impartiality means in a polarised world

item.title: Quinn Emanuel raises pay for junior lawyers in London to £189,000
item.description: Salary increase signals elite law firms are on cusp of fresh pay war as competition intensifies

item.title: The Musk exception on banking fees for SpaceX
item.description: Most companies are still likely to pay full price on smaller US deals as a prestige good

item.title: Chip designer Raspberry Pi surges by a fifth on profitability upgrades
item.description: Cambridge-based company expects ‘robust demand’ for its products to push unit sales above 4mn in first half

item.title: William Hill owner agrees to £243mn offer from gaming group Bally’s Intralot
item.description: Evoke put itself up for sale after taking hit from steep UK tax rises

item.title: Apollo drops £1.5bn bid for London-listed Bodycote
item.description: US private equity group says it may make another offer for components maker in coming months

item.title: The CPG blues
item.description: Bad luck and bad decisions in consumer products

item.title: FirstFT: SpaceX lines up retail investors for record IPO allocation
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Mythos at NSA and PwC partner payouts

item.title: Wall Street’s red carpet for SpaceX
item.description: Banks and stock exchanges have bent over backwards for the historic IPO of Elon Musk’s rocket company

item.title: Inside the CBS mutiny against Bari Weiss and David Ellison
item.description: A saga that started with Donald Trump’s assault on ‘60 Minutes’ has turned into a full-blown crisis

item.title: Heineken investors push struggling brewer to hire outsider as chief
item.description: Family behind Dutch group under pressure to ditch custom of promoting from within as CEO search drags on

item.title: How much value is AI really creating?
item.description: Eye-opening changes to the speed and volume of work are not always translating into genuine productivity

item.title: Rolls-Royce under fire for outsourcing parts of UK nuclear project to South Korea
item.description: Multibillion-pound contract to build three small modular reactors was signed with government body in April

item.title: Is the K-shaped economy real? Cut-price UK retailers should hope not
item.description: Selling goods for rock-bottom prices can be surprisingly profitable and may entice middle-tier chains to muscle in on discount stores

item.title: Trump lawyers refuse to reveal financial information to BBC in $10bn lawsuit
item.description: Broadcaster has sought documents under subpoena in effort to identify impact of ‘Panorama’ documentary on US president

item.title: Backlash over plan to drop US quarterly reporting demands is building
item.description: Enraged investors have sent flood of letters to SEC to express their opposition

item.title: PwC cuts partner payouts in China Evergrande fallout
item.description: Proceeds from a 2022 disposal will no longer be distributed as firm deals with fines and looming lawsuit

item.title: Elon Musk’s SpaceX lines up retail investors for record IPO allocation
item.description: Up to a quarter of the rocket builder’s $75bn float will be set aside for individual investors

item.title: Podcast: change management in an era of AI disruption
item.description: Listen to a conversation between FT journalists and business school specialists about how business leaders should respond to new technology

item.title: Did BP miss CRH warning signs before hiring Albert Manifold?
item.description: Former colleagues say businessman’s management style should have raised concerns as oil group weighed him as chair

item.title: Oxford BioMedica leaves door open to private equity takeover
item.description: UK-listed group has already rejected multiple unsolicited offers from EQT and says a deal would have to be the right fit

item.title: Hedge funds bet against call centre stocks as AI threat grows
item.description: Outsourcing companies hit as investors see ‘clean’ disruption risk

item.title: Humanoids bring AI’s creative destruction to the shop floor
item.description: The anxiety of those whose jobs are at risk won’t be enough to stave off the march of the androids
